Metadata.title: JRE Global Home — Japan Real Estate for Foreigners
Metadata.description: Rent, buy, short-term, sell, management and office. Tokyo real estate guided in KO/EN/JP by JRE Global Home.

Hero.title: Your Tokyo living starts with JRE Global Home
Hero.subtitle: Japan real estate for foreigners — from rentals to offices, guided in your language.
Hero.searchNote: Results are served by our listings app (list.japan-real.com).

ServicesSection.heading: Services
ServicesSection.subheading: Every step of Japanese real estate, in one place.

desc.rent: Foreigner-friendly listings with daily-updated rental search.
desc.buy: Guidance through financing, contract and closing.

desc.sell: Safe selling with local expertise and marketing.

rent.tagline: Renting with confidence, even as a foreigner
rent.intro: Guarantors, upfront costs, language barriers — we clear the hurdles of renting as a foreigner and match you with a home from daily-updated listings.
features: Filter by foreigner-friendly, pet-friendly and more
Guidance on guarantor companies and initial costs

buy.tagline: Buying Japanese property, start to finish
buy.intro: Non-residents can buy property in Japan too. We guide you through selection, financing, contract, registration and closing.
features: Important-matters explanation (重説) in your language

shortTerm.intro: Fully furnished short-term rentals for business trips, study, or while preparing to relocate — contracted easily online.

sell.intro: We support your sale with local market analysis and multilingual marketing — remote selling for non-resident owners included.

management.intro: From tenant sourcing and screening to rent collection and restoration, we manage your property on your behalf.

office.intro: Office space and registered addresses for companies entering Japan, with company-formation partners on hand.

About.heading: A partner you can trust
About.body: JRE Global Home is operated by WINBRO LLC, based in Shinjuku, Tokyo. Our licensed real-estate professionals (宅建士) support foreign clients through renting and buying, from start to finish.
About.license: Licensed real-estate broker · WINBRO LLC (Shinjuku, Tokyo)

one.quote: Settled in Tokyo without language worries. They walked me through every step of the contract.
one.author: K.L., Korea

two.quote: Found a pet-friendly apartment in Minato within a week. Highly recommend.
two.author: M.S., USA

one.q: Can foreigners buy property in Japan?
one.a: Yes. Japan places no restrictions on foreign property ownership, and non-residents can purchase too. Financing terms are advised case by case.

two.q: Do you offer support in English/Korean?
two.a: Yes. We support you in KO, EN and JP, from first inquiry to signing.

three.q: How much are the agency fees?
three.a: By Japanese custom, rentals are typically one month's rent (+tax); sales are calculated on the transaction value. We disclose fees clearly upfront.

four.a: We focus on Tokyo's 23 wards and cover listings across the greater Tokyo area.

ContactPage.heading: Contact us
ContactPage.subheading: Reach us by email or KakaoTalk. We reply in Korean, English and Japanese.
ContactPage.responseNote: We reply in order during business hours.

AboutPage.heading: About us
AboutPage.lead: JRE Global Home is a foreigner-focused real-estate service operated by WINBRO LLC in Shinjuku, Tokyo. We guide you through finding a home in Japan from start to finish, without language or system barriers.
AboutPage.valueBusiness: Licensed real-estate broker (宅地建物取引業)

one.title: No language worries
one.body: Support in three languages, from inquiry to contract.

two.title: Transparent costs
two.body: Fees and initial costs disclosed clearly upfront.

three.title: Properly licensed
three.body: Handled directly by licensed professionals (宅建士).

minato.intro: Tokyo's premier residential district, home to embassies and global firms. Includes popular Roppongi, Azabu and Shirokane.
highlights: Near foreign communities and international schools

shibuya.intro: A hub of shopping and culture with dense IT presence — a lively mix of residential and commercial.
highlights: Stylish neighborhoods like Daikanyama and Ebisu
Close to startups and creative jobs

shinjuku.intro: A transport hub and home to JRE's head office, where offices and homes coexist with superb access.
highlights: Japan's busiest hub station, nationwide access
Wide range of sizes and budgets

meguro.intro: A calm, refined residential area famous for the Nakameguro cherry blossoms — popular with families and couples.

setagaya.intro: Tokyo's most populous ward, with spacious neighborhoods and parks — great for families settling in.

bunkyo.intro: A center of education and academia including the University of Tokyo — quiet and safe, popular with students and families.
highlights: Culture and greenery like Koishikawa Korakuen

GuidePage.heading: Living Info
GuidePage.subheading: Everything you need to set up daily life when starting out in Japan.

electricity.title: Electricity setup
electricity.intro: Apply to your power company to start service on your move-in date — easily done online or by phone.
steps: Choose and apply to a power company
Enter move-in date and account details
Flip the breaker and start using

gas.title: Gas setup
gas.intro: Gas requires an attended activation for safety — book before you move in.
steps: Book activation with the gas company

water.title: Water setup
water.intro: Apply to the water bureau to start service — usually no attendance needed.

internet.title: Internet setup
internet.intro: After choosing a line and provider, installation may need booking — apply with plenty of lead time.
steps: Set up the router and connect

bank.title: Opening a bank account
bank.intro: Open an account with your residence card and seal — needed for salary and rent transfers.

mobile.title: Mobile phone setup
mobile.intro: Sign up with a carrier or a low-cost SIM (格安SIM). ID and a payment method are required.

BlogPage.subheading: The JRE blog covering area guides and living info.

shinjukuVsShibuya.title: Shinjuku vs Shibuya: where should you live?
shinjukuVsShibuya.excerpt: Comparing two popular areas by transport, price and vibe.
shinjukuVsShibuya.body: Shinjuku is a transport hub with nationwide access, while Shibuya offers a trendy atmosphere and proximity to IT and creative jobs. Choose based on your budget and lifestyle.

utilitiesGuide.title: The complete guide to electricity, gas & water in Japan
utilitiesGuide.excerpt: The utility setup order you must know before moving in.
utilitiesGuide.body: Gas needs an attended activation, so book ahead; electricity and water can be started simply online. Applying ahead of your move-in date means no trouble from day one.

internetForForeigners.title: How foreigners can set up home internet
internetForForeigners.excerpt: Step by step, from choosing a line to booking installation.
internetForForeigners.body: Decide on a line and provider first; if installation is needed, slots can fill up, so apply early. For short stays, a home router with no installation is a good option.
